summary: This is a very brief teaser post where Joel Spolsky shares the opening of his foreword to Eric Sink's book 'Eric Sink on the Business of Software.' The excerpt begins an anecdote about Joel's first business venture at age fourteen, involving a coffee station and donuts at a university summer institute. The post serves primarily as a promotional link to the full foreword rather than developing a complete argument.
key_points: Joel wrote the foreword for Eric Sink's book on the business of software
The foreword opens with a personal anecdote about Joel's earliest entrepreneurial experience at age fourteen
The post is essentially a teaser linking to the full foreword text
money_quotes: There was a big urn full of coffee next to the desk, and if you wanted coffee, you helped yourself and left a quarter in a little cup. I didn't drink coffee, myself, but I *did* like donuts and thought some nice donuts would go well with the coffee.
key_insight: This brief teaser post promotes Joel's foreword to Eric Sink's book by opening with a charming anecdote about his first entrepreneurial venture at age fourteen.
